Inter-rater reliability (R step of CRAFT)

Source: R/reliability.R
reliab.Rd

Unified interface to common inter-rater reliability metrics, so the researcher can pick a method appropriate to their data. The method argument chooses among:

Usage

reliab(
  ratings,
  method = c("auto", "cohen", "weighted", "fleiss", "kripp", "icc", "agree"),
  level = c("nominal", "ordinal", "interval", "ratio"),
  weights = c("equal", "squared"),
  ...
)

Arguments

ratings

A data frame or matrix where each column is a rater (annotator / model) and each row is an item. Missing values are permitted by kripp.alpha but not by the others.

method

One of "cohen", "weighted", "fleiss", "kripp", "icc", "agree". The default "auto" picks "cohen" for 2 raters + nominal data, "weighted" for 2 raters + ordinal data, "fleiss" for >2 raters + nominal data, "kripp" otherwise.

level

Measurement level: "nominal", "ordinal", "interval", or "ratio". Used by "auto", "weighted", and "kripp".

weights

For method = "weighted": either "equal" (linear) or "squared" (quadratic).

...

Passed to the underlying irr function (e.g. type, model for ICC).

Value

A one-row data frame.

Details

"cohen"

Cohen's kappa. Two raters, nominal data. Wraps irr::kappa2(weight = "unweighted").

"weighted"

Weighted Cohen's kappa. Two raters, ordinal data. Wraps irr::kappa2() with weight = "equal" (linear) or "squared" (quadratic) via the weights argument.

"fleiss"

Fleiss' kappa. More than two raters, nominal data. Wraps irr::kappam.fleiss().

"kripp"

Krippendorff's alpha. Any number of raters, missing values allowed, any measurement level. Wraps irr::kripp.alpha() with method controlled by the level argument ("nominal", "ordinal", "interval", "ratio").

"icc"

Intra-class correlation. Continuous ratings. Wraps irr::icc(). Use type and model arguments for the ICC form.

"agree"

Simple percent agreement. Wraps irr::agree(). Reported alongside other metrics; not a substitute for chance-corrected agreement.

All methods return a tidy one-row data frame with columns method, value, n_raters, n_items, and any method-specific fields (e.g., p_value, ci_lower, ci_upper when available).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
  ratings <- data.frame(
    human  = c("sup", "opp", "sup", "non"),
    gpt5   = c("sup", "opp", "sup", "non"),
    gemini = c("sup", "opp", "non", "non")
  )
  reliab(ratings, method = "kripp", level = "nominal")
  reliab(ratings[, 1:2], method = "cohen")
  reliab(ratings, method = "fleiss")
} # }
